definition: The blocker requires a specific single-byte or small-literal value; context-sensitive coverage retains structurally-shaped intermediate corpus members (IHDR-valid, format-valid) as distinct coverage events, providing havoc with many more opportunities to mutate the target byte into the correct value.

authoring_contract.goal: ONE parameterized template.c isolating this cluster's shared mechanism, with exactly ONE compile-time -D knob = the program-feature axis. params.json:parameter MUST name that macro; scan_values sweep it; fuzzers = involved_fuzzers; expected_direction = '<winner> > <loser>'.
authoring_contract.isolate: No partial-match coverage edges unless the mechanism IS a gradient (VP families). Keep the gate the only objective.

signature.mechanism_summary: CtxHook indexes IHDR-validation edges by call context, so each distinct caller path to OSS_FUZZ_png_check_IHDR is a new coverage bucket, preserving more intermediate IHDR-shaped inputs and raising the chance havoc assembles the forbidden color_type/bit_depth combination.
signature.one_line: call-context edge indexing retains diverse IHDR-parsing intermediates to eventually assemble forbidden combination

analysis.summary_one_line: IHDR color_type/bit_depth bytes must form an individually-valid but mutually-disallowed combination (e.g. PALETTE with depth>8, or RGB/GA/RGBA with depth<8)

hypotheses.what_input_feature: Same valid-but-disallowed color_type/bit_depth combination at offsets 0x18/0x19 reaching L2636 TRUE; no extra byte requirement beyond naive's.
hypotheses.why_winner_satisfies: naive_ctx resolves on all 10 trials; its context-indexed edge map records OSS_FUZZ_png_check_IHDR edges under distinct call contexts, retaining more intermediate IHDR-parsing inputs and preserving the corpus diversity that eventually produces the forbidden combination.
hypotheses.why_loser_doesnt: naive collapses all call paths into bare edges, so context-distinct IHDR explorations dedup to the same bucket and are discarded; it stays blocked 9/10 trials and never assembles the combination.
hypotheses.mechanism_attribution: naive_ctx's CtxHook folds the caller chain into the edge-map index so the same IHDR-validation edge is new coverage under a new call context; this finer feedback granularity preserves more partial inputs and increases the chance of stumbling onto the disallowed color_type/bit_depth bytes.

evidence_trail.claim: The blocker is the TRUE side of the L2636 color_type/bit_depth combination check.
The TRUE side fires for PALETTE+depth>8 or for RGB/GRAY_ALPHA/RGB_ALPHA with depth<8.
Winners take L2636 TRUE (T=45) while losers never do (T=0).
evidence_trail.exact_quote: T=45 F=0  T=0 F=17  if (((color_type == PNG_COLOR_TYPE_PALETTE) && bit_depth ...  <-- BLOCKER

evidence_trail.claim: color_type and bit_depth pass their individual validity gates at L2622 and L2629 before reaching L2636.
cmplog wins over naive with the I2S delta, resolving 10/10 vs 1/10.
cmplog's I2SRandReplace substitutes one logged CMP operand at matching input offsets.
evidence_trail.exact_quote:
scans the input for byte sequences matching one
operand, and substitutes the other operand at those offsets

evidence_trail.claim: value_profile wins over naive with the value_profile delta.
value_profile preserves inputs whose CMP operands are closer to matching as new CMP_MAP buckets.
evidence_trail.exact_quote:
An input that produces a
CMP-operand pair closer to matching than any previously-seen pair
adds a new CMP_MAP bucket and is preserved as corpus.

evidence_trail.claim: naive_ctx wins over naive with the ctx_coverage delta.
naive_ctx's CtxHook records the same edge at different map slots per call path.
evidence_trail.exact_quote:
the same basic-block edge is
recorded at different map slots depending on the call path that
reached it

evidence_trail.claim: The discriminating bytes are at offsets 0x18 (bit_depth) and 0x19 (color_type) where W and L differ.

falsifiability.would_be_refuted_by: A synthetic harness gating only on a valid-but-disallowed color_type/bit_depth pairing where cmplog, value_profile, and naive_ctx all fail to outperform naive (no resolution advantage) would refute that these three techniques each enable the forbidden-combination branch.

analysis.weakest_evidence_point: The naive_ctx (ctx_coverage) advantage is the least mechanistically tied to the byte feature — its win may reflect general corpus-retention diversity rather than anything specific to the color_type/bit_depth combination, and its hit_div is only 7 vs 144/103 for the CMP techniques.

signature.mechanism_summary: CtxHook folds call context into edge-map index so reaching the PNG_COLOR_TYPE_PALETTE case via a new caller chain is new coverage; this preserves more IHDR-shaped corpus entries whose havoc mutation can land 0x03 at color_type offset 0x19.
signature.one_line: context-indexed edges preserve IHDR-shaped inputs until havoc places palette color_type byte

analysis.summary_one_line: IHDR color_type byte at offset 0x19 must equal 0x03 (PNG_COLOR_TYPE_PALETTE) to take the true side.

hypotheses.what_input_feature: The same color_type byte at offset 0x19 must equal 0x03 to enter the line 883 case body; no new comparison structure, just the same equality.
hypotheses.why_winner_satisfies: naive_ctx resolves 10/10 trials and its winner-resolving seeds (the displayed naive_ctx seeds) all carry 0x03 at offset 0x19.
hypotheses.why_loser_doesnt: naive blocks 9/10 with non-3 color_type bytes; the same havoc mutator stack as naive_ctx but flat edge indexing leaves it stuck.
hypotheses.mechanism_attribution: naive_ctx uses naive's identical havoc/token mutators (no I2S, no CMP_MAP), but the CtxHook folds call-context into the edge map so reaching line 883 via a new caller chain registers as new coverage; this richer corpus-retention keeps more IHDR-shaped intermediates alive, raising the odds a havoc mutation lands 0x03 at offset 0x19.

evidence_trail.claim: The blocker is the PNG_COLOR_TYPE_PALETTE case of the switch on png_ptr->color_type, true side globally blocked.
color_type is read from buf[9], which is the IHDR payload byte at input offset 0x19.
The winning inputs hold 0x03 at offset 0x19 while losers hold 00/04/06.
Line 883 fires only for the winner set; losers never take its true side.

falsifiability.would_be_refuted_by: A synthetic harness gating on color_type==3 where cmplog still blocks while naive resolves at the same rate — i.e. forcing 0x03 at offset 0x19 fails to flip the branch, showing offset 0x19 is not the controlling byte.

analysis.weakest_evidence_point: The naive_ctx win is attributed to richer corpus retention raising havoc luck rather than any comparison-solving, which is the least mechanistically direct of the three claims.

signature.mechanism_summary: CtxHook makes the same sBIT-handler edge new coverage under each distinct call path, retaining more intermediate inputs whose IHDR color_type can be havoc-mutated toward the palette value 3; flat-edge naive collapses these paths and discards the intermediates.
signature.one_line: context-indexed edges retain near-palette IHDR inputs until havoc places color_type=3

analysis.summary_one_line: IHDR color_type byte (offset 0x19) must equal PNG_COLOR_TYPE_PALETTE (3) so the sBIT handler takes the palette branch.

hypotheses.what_input_feature: The same IHDR color_type byte at offset 0x19 must equal 0x03; naive_ctx differs from naive only in context-sensitive edge indexing, not in any comparison-solving stage.
hypotheses.why_winner_satisfies: naive_ctx resolves in 9/10 trials (avg hitcount 39); all winner-resolving seeds shown are fuzzer=naive_ctx and carry 03 at offset 0x19, and context-indexed edges distinguish the call paths through png_handle_sBIT, retaining more intermediate corpus entries that carry the palette color_type forward.
hypotheses.why_loser_doesnt: plain naive folds the same edges into a single map slot regardless of call context, so context-distinct paths that preserve a near-palette IHDR are not separately rewarded, and naive stays blocked in 9/10 trials.
hypotheses.mechanism_attribution: naive_ctx's CtxHook folds the caller chain into the edge-map index so the same edge counts as new coverage when reached via a different call context; this finer feedback retains more corpus diversity (including inputs whose IHDR color_type can be mutated toward 3) but uses the identical havoc/token mutator stack as naive with no CMP introspection.

evidence_trail.claim: The blocker is an equality test of png_ptr->color_type against PNG_COLOR_TYPE_PALETTE, true side globally blocked.
The winning byte value at IHDR offset 0x19 is 0x03 across all winner samples while losers never carry 0x03 there.
cmplog wins the branch in 10/10 trials versus naive's 1/10 with a strong probability divergence.

falsifiability.would_be_refuted_by: A synthetic harness gating on color_type==3 in which cmplog does NOT resolve the branch faster than naive (no dose response to the equality-gate width), which would show I2S substitution is not the operative mechanism.

analysis.weakest_evidence_point: The ctx_coverage hypothesis attributes the win to context-indexed corpus retention, but the prompt offers no direct evidence that context-distinct paths through png_handle_sBIT are what preserve near-palette IHDR inputs; it is inferred from mechanism description rather than seed lineage.

signature.mechanism_summary: CtxHook folds caller chain into edge-map index so reaching the bKGD palette comparison via a new call context is new coverage, preserving intermediate inputs whose havoc mutation can land color_type=3 at offset 0x19; naive lacks this retention gradient.
signature.one_line: context-indexed edges retain IHDR intermediates until havoc places palette color_type=3 for bKGD

analysis.summary_one_line: IHDR color_type byte (offset 0x19) must equal 3 (PNG_COLOR_TYPE_PALETTE) so the bKGD out-of-place check takes the true side.

hypotheses.what_input_feature: The IHDR color_type byte at offset 0x19 must equal 3 so the bKGD handler reaches the palette-out-of-place body; the same edge is newly rewarded under a distinct call context.
hypotheses.why_winner_satisfies: naive_ctx resolves 10/10 and its winner-resolving seeds (stored under naive_ctx) carry 08 03 at 0x18-0x19, taking the W-only out-of-place body at L1934-L1938.
hypotheses.why_loser_doesnt: naive (no context indexing) keeps color_type at 06/04/00, never produces 0x03, and blocks the branch in 9/10 trials with avg duration 21.60h.
hypotheses.mechanism_attribution: naive_ctx's CtxHook folds the caller chain into the edge-map index, so reaching the bKGD comparison via a new call context counts as new coverage; this preserves intermediate inputs that naive discards, retaining more parent material whose havoc mutation eventually lands color_type=3 at offset 0x19.

cmplog winning seeds carry 08 03 at offsets 0x18-0x19.
A loser-blocking naive seed carries 08 06 at offsets 0x18-0x19 (color_type 6).

falsifiability.would_be_refuted_by: A synthetic harness gating on color_type == 3 in which cmplog resolves no faster than naive (no I2S advantage), or in which winners reach the branch with color_type bytes other than 0x03, would refute the offset-0x19==3 feature hypothesis.

analysis.weakest_evidence_point: The value_profile and naive_ctx attributions are inferred from mechanism descriptions and the shared 0x19==3 byte requirement; no value_profile/naive_ctx lineage was run to confirm those techniques (not I2S leakage) drove their wins.

signature.mechanism_summary: CtxHook folds caller-chain context into edge slots, retaining more chunk-valid intermediates from which havoc can mutate color_type to 3; naive's flat edge map gives no extra credit for distinct call contexts.
signature.one_line: Context-sensitive coverage retains PNG chunk intermediates reaching color_type==3

analysis.summary_one_line: IHDR color_type byte at offset 0x19 must equal 3 (PNG_COLOR_TYPE_PALETTE) to take the true branch.

hypotheses.what_input_feature: The IHDR color_type byte at offset 0x19 must equal 0x03 to take the true branch at line 276 inside OSS_FUZZ_png_set_IHDR.
hypotheses.why_winner_satisfies: Winner seeds carry 0x03 at offset 0x19 (W bytes 03(.)x30); naive_ctx indexes the same edges by call context, so reaching png_set_IHDR via additional call paths registers new (context,edge) coverage and rewards intermediate inputs that retain more PNG chunk structure.
hypotheses.why_loser_doesnt: Loser seeds never set color_type to 3 (L bytes 00(.)x7 04(.)x2 06(.)x1); naive's flat edge map gives no extra credit for distinct call contexts, so it preserves fewer structurally-valid intermediates that could be mutated into the PALETTE value.
hypotheses.mechanism_attribution: naive_ctx's CtxHook folds the caller chain into the edge-map index so the same edge counts as new coverage under different call contexts; this finer feedback retains more chunk-valid corpus members from which havoc reaches color_type==3, whereas naive's bare-edge map does not.

falsifiability.would_be_refuted_by: A synthetic harness with an equality gate color_type==3 on a single input byte where cmplog does NOT resolve the gate faster than naive (no prob_div / time advantage), which would refute the I2S-substitution mechanism.

analysis.weakest_evidence_point: The value_profile and naive_ctx hypotheses share the same single-byte equality feature as I2S, so their distinct mechanism attributions rest on technique knowledge rather than seed-byte evidence that uniquely separates the three winners.

signature.mechanism_summary: Context-sensitive coverage indexes the comment-body edge by caller-chain hash, so a tab byte inside a comment earns a fresh (call-context, edge) bucket that bare-edge naive discards.
signature.one_line: 0x09 tab in comment body; ctx retains seeds via call-context edge indexing

analysis.summary_one_line: A well-formed XML comment opener <!-- whose body contains a literal 0x09 tab byte, so the loop's third disjunct fires.

hypotheses.what_input_feature: A comment body (after a <!-- opener) containing a literal 0x09 tab byte at a position not already matched by the >0x2D or 0x20..0x2C disjuncts, i.e. the byte must equal exactly 0x09.
hypotheses.why_winner_satisfies: All five winner-resolving seeds are naive_ctx and carry 0x09 bytes interleaved with the comment opener (e.g. seed 1 has bytes '09 09' at 0x07-0x08 and a '<!--------' run followed by '09 09 09'); context-sensitive coverage lets naive_ctx retain the intermediate seeds that built this <!--+tab structure as new (call-context, edge) coverage.
hypotheses.why_loser_doesnt: Plain naive shares the identical havoc/token mutator set but only a bare per-edge bucket, so the partial-progress comment seeds collapse to already-covered edges and are discarded; the loser-blocking naive seeds contain '--' runs but no 0x09 tab inside a comment body, so L4985 is taken only on its False side (T=0).
hypotheses.mechanism_attribution: context-sensitive coverage: naive_ctx's CtxHook folds the caller chain into the edge-map index, so reaching xmlParseComment's content loop via a new call path counts as new coverage and preserves the staging seeds that bare naive throws away.

evidence_trail.claim: The blocker is the third disjunct *in == 0x09 of the comment-content while loop, whose globally blocked side is True.
0x09 is below the 0x20 lower bound of the second disjunct, so the byte must be exactly a tab to reach the True side.
naive_ctx is the winner and naive the loser in the ctx_coverage pair, with naive_ctx resolving 10/10 and naive only 2/10.
Winner-resolving seeds are all naive_ctx and carry 0x09 tab bytes alongside comment-opener bytes.
Loser-blocking naive seeds contain '--' runs but the dumped bytes show no tab inside a comment body.
naive_ctx's CtxHook folds the call context into the edge-map index so the same edge is recorded at different slots per call path.
evidence_trail.exact_quote:
folds it into the edge-map index, so the same basic-block edge is
recorded at different map slots depending on the call path that
reached it.

naive has no I2S and only havoc+token mutators.
evidence_trail.exact_quote: **Observed `mutation_op` in seed metadata**: any of the above. No I2S.

evidence_trail.claim: grimoire loses to cmplog despite both running the I2S stage; grimoire adds GeneralizationStage and Grimoire mutators.
grimoire's GeneralizationStage replaces concrete byte runs with <GAP> placeholders by repeated re-execution.
evidence_trail.exact_quote: replaces concrete byte runs in a corpus entry with `<GAP>` placeholders

evidence_trail.claim: grimoire incurs extra executions during generalization, each gap validated by a re-run.
evidence_trail.exact_quote:
plus extra executions
during generalization (each candidate gap is validated by a re-run).

evidence_trail.claim: The loser-blocking grimoire seeds were produced by Grimoire structural mutators.

falsifiability.would_be_refuted_by: A synthetic comment-content gate testing a byte-exact 0x09 tab where cmplog does NOT outpace naive, and grimoire matches or beats cmplog, and naive_ctx shows no advantage over naive — i.e. all three deltas vanish, showing the divergence is not technique-driven.

analysis.weakest_evidence_point: The cmplog>naive I2S attribution is unverified in lineage because only naive_ctx winner seeds are stored, so I2S's role rests on mechanism reasoning rather than a confirmed dash-row ancestor.
